title: Cacalia remotiflora
taxonomy: Cacalia remotiflora is a plant species classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, specifically within the order Asterales. The genus Cacalia is a part of the tribe Cacalieae and is native to East Asia. The species is further categorized as Cacalia remotiflora, indicating its unique characteristics and distinct features. Through its classification, Cacalia remotiflora can be identified and distinguished from other plant species within the same family.
uses: Cacalia remotiflora is known to have several medicinal uses. Cacalia remotiflora is traditionally used in some Asian cultures to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and applications of Cacalia remotiflora.
